behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Escalation evidence: "Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition." This depends on real git rename semantics and CLI execution against an actual repo. A mocked git response can hide failures in subprocess invocation, porcelain/plumbing parsing, or rename-threshold behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior text is purely git/CLI counting, with no AI/ML decision steps.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit baseline, perform a file rename with content changes near rename threshold, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert stdout report has the file only in renamed (not added/deleted).

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Escalation evidence: "Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output." Real binary diff handling is tied to actual git subprocess output and filesystem content; mocks often miss encoding/stream edge cases that cause real crashes. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model output quality or multi-agent sequence is referenced.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., image bytes), run the CLI subprocess, verify exit code is success and stdout includes binary file count/category without traceback.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Escalation evidence: "Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error." This hinges on real git behavior in an unborn-HEAD repository and actual CLI process/error handling. In-process fakes can miss real exit codes/stderr patterns. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior text contains no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, invoke the CLI subprocess, assert success exit code and empty structured stdout report (not error output).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Escalation evidence: "match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed." Verifying against actual history requires real commits/diffs and CLI interaction with git/OS state. Mocked history fixtures can hide failures in revision range selection, rename detection, and subprocess parsing. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no LLM or sequential agent pipeline in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known added/deleted/renamed/modified events across several commits, run CLI subprocess over that repo, and assert each reported count matches the known ground truth.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Escalation evidence: "output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged." Staged vs unstaged is a real git index/working-tree boundary; failures appear with real repo/index behavior and subprocess calls. Fakes frequently miss index state nuances. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior is deterministic CLI/git state comparison, not AI-driven.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create equivalent change sets once as unstaged and once staged, run the CLI subprocess in both states, and assert stdout reports are byte-identical.
